item.title: Can Imgur help me in remembering my brother?
item.description:
I lost my brother last weekend... He was severely disabled with epilepsy, but he was always happy and laughing. His condition was chronic so over the years he lost the ability to walk and talk. And yet he was always full of joy; grinning and laughing when you would talk to him. He would smack things with his hand as a way of interacting with his surroundings so we would have lots of fun doing high-fives.
Its hard looking back on all the memories, but I know its really important to process through the grief. I know Imgur is a big mix of lots of beliefs and backgrounds; my family is Christian and it helps to picture him in Heaven in a healed and healthy body. When he was in hospice, I kept imagining him being able to run through green fields and play and laugh. The Bible talks about the Earth being redeemed and being made perfect again (we can all agree things are pretty messed up now, right) so its nice to think about him enjoying Heaven by doing all the things he wasn't able to in this life.
There are so many talented artists on here so I was hoping I could get some help with making a memorial picture/painting of him. I have this image of him laughing and running through green, grassy fields. Would any artistic imgurians be willing to help me make a painting that I can remember him by? I have included some pictures from his life that I hope will help. If more would be useful, I am currently going through our old photos and making digital copies. Hopefully, I will have more soon. Thanks in advance
See you on the other side Mathew! EDIT: Wow, thank you everyone for your support and kind words! I am so happy to see that Matthew's smile continues to reach out and brighten people's day even after he is gone! Its so heartwarming to know that this community can carry on his memory!
item.dc:creator: chillychiweenie

item.title: Okayama, Japan.
item.description: Okayama is the capital city of it's namesake prefecture, in western Japan. It’s known for the 16th-century Okayama Castle, dubbed “Crow Castle” for its black exterior, and for Korakuen, an iconic formal garden. The Hayashibara Museum of Art shows East Asian art and artifacts. The Okayama Prefectural Museum of Art displays regional works. To the west, 15th-century Kibitsu-jinja Shrine features a long, covered walkway.
item.dc:creator: dolphinyaso

item.title: Scenes from the National Nurses' March
item.description:
Nurses have been planning this day for months to unify for the purpose of supporting safe staffing/ratios, supporting measures to ensure hospitals support safety/ violence reduction for staff. opposing state-supported caps on nurse pay ("free market for me, not for thee"), and reducing disparities and racial prejudice in the profession. Met these great ladies right off the train station! They approved of my sign.
Got there a little late due to a work meeting, but I made it!
Back of the sign, admittedly stolen from one of Orisa's new voice lines in Overwatch 2 but it's exactly what I wanted to say!
Nurse Nander delivered a great speech!
Loved these ladies. Impactful message.
Grateful for my brothers and sisters for speaking loudly and clearly! Our profession has historical defects that we must contend with.
Love the spice (JCAHO, a.k.a. Joint Commission, is supposed to audit safe practices in hospitals...curiously, they were nowhere to be found or heard when nurses raised the alarm about unsafe conditions during the pandemic!)
We honor our fallen through advocacy and action.
All too real. Try to imagine bathing, toileting, feeding, giving medicine, assessing, and comforting 40 people in 12h...pro tip: it is not humanly possible.
One of our recurring themes: we are humans, and as such, require conditions conducive to humans, NOT superheroes.
We came here for a reason.
item.dc:creator: Guluzinyu

item.dc:creator: JustineFromG2A

item.title: Life sucks need to vent
item.description:
After years of fertility issues and treatments we were finally going to have our baby. Catch covid just as we started to get excited and start buying stuff. Too much shit has happened for us not to be pessimistic hence why we took so long to start buying stuff.
Luckily still entitled to maternity leave when you get that far into a pregnancy in Scotland so took some of that time to come to terms with what had happened and make sure I was mentally able to go back to work. After 1 week of reduced hours back at work, test comes back positive.
After my isolation I went back to work at my usual 40 hrs. Gran dies that week. She had really bad dementia and it got to the point where we’re just relieved she’s not in pain anymore, we were all still heartbroken though.
Funeral was on Tuesday and had a much harder time coping with it than I thought I would. Got the news this morning that my mum’s friend died last night. My mum had to watch her mother die last Wednesday then was with her friend at the hospital when she died just 1 week later. It’s been a shit time lately and I’m 100% sure my mum isn’t coping but she’s adamant she’s fine. Trying to be there for her as much as I can until she comes to terms with everything that has happened. Here’s a puppy
item.dc:creator: MissDisney91
